page: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, virtualized via Microsoft Hyper-V. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz, configured with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs. The memory allocation is exceptionally high, providing 1344.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 42.0 GB per physical core. Additionally, the instance includes 3780 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, though it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the processor demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core, image processing, and text processing scores placing in the top 10% tier. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth read and write operations rank in the top 25%, reflecting high memory throughput. Multi-core performance is also strong, as evidenced by Passmark CPU and Geekbench multi-core scores. However, while prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation using large 70B models is weak, falling into the bottom 25% tier due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio and large local storage capacity provide a dense resource profile for memory-intensive workloads without the overhead of higher vCPU counts. This makes the instance suitable for large in-memory databases, heavy data caching, and memory-bound enterprise applications. It is also well-suited for memory-intensive data processing and single-threaded tasks that benefit from high clock speeds, though it is less optimal for massive parallel GPU-accelerated tasks or large-scale LLM text generation.
description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs. It features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 1344.0 GB (42.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local storage. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, ranking in the top 10% in Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks, alongside strong uncached memory bandwidth. While multi-core and cryptographic performance are strong, the lack of a GPU results in weak text generation speeds for large language models. This instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, data caching, and enterprise applications requiring high memory density per vCPU.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring a 32-vCPU Intel Xeon processor, 1344 GB of RAM, and 3780 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 1344 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized instance featuring dedicated high-frequency Intel Xeon processing and substantial local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
1344.0 GB of RAM providing a high 42.0 GB per core memory density
3780 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks
Strong uncached memory bandwidth and multi-core processing capabilities
Best suited for in-memory databases, data caching, and memory-intensive enterprise applications
